source.title: Thalamic Spindles Promote Memory Formation during Sleep through Triple Phase-Locking of Cortical, Thalamic, and Hippocampal Rhythms

confidence_rationale: Closed-loop optogenetics in mice establishes a causal gating relation: only thalamic spindles phase-locked to the cortical slow-oscillation up-state increase triple (SO-spindle-ripple) coupling and boost hippocampus-dependent memory. In-phase vs out-of-phase stimulation is a direct test of the 'phase of the carrier rhythm selects the target' hypothesis that MECH-261 instantiates as dict-keyed write gates over operating modes. Mapping from 'select which cortical site sees a ripple-delivered replay' to MECH-261's 'select which SD-033 subdivision receives the write' is close but not identity -- the paper demonstrates temporal gating of propagation rather than target-selective routing per se.
failure_signatures: Out-of-phase spindle stimulation increased spindle rate without increasing memory consolidation, showing that mere presence of a spindle does not gate the write -- the phase relation to the cortical slow oscillation is what matters. An implementation that raises spindle density without respecting the phase geometry will fail to consolidate.

Triple phase-locking selectively boosted hippocampus-dependent memory; non-hippocampal control tasks did not show the same enhancement. A write-gate that is operating-mode-selective but ignores the circuit-level selectivity of the carrier rhythms will over-generalise.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Thalamic sleep spindles phase-locked in-phase with cortical slow-oscillation up-states produce triple coupling of slow oscillation, spindle, and hippocampal ripple, and this triple-coupled geometry is causally necessary for hippocampus-dependent memory consolidation.
mapping.ree_translation: The biological substrate for MECH-261's operating-mode write-gate is not a single mode scalar but a multi-oscillation phase relationship. In SWS, the gate that decides 'should this hippocampal replay write into this cortical subdivision' is carried by the SO-spindle phase geometry -- the thalamus effectively time-windows the cortical site to be receptive to the ripple. In the REE dict-keyed write gate abstraction, this is the physical realisation of the 'sws_schema' mode gate multiplying content written to sd_033a (lateral-PFC-analog) vs not-written elsewhere.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Latchoumane demonstrate gating of consolidation magnitude, not explicit routing to a specific cortical subdivision. Evidence that SWS-phase-coupling selects the lateral-PFC target specifically rather than cortex more broadly comes from Maingret 2016 and Helfrich 2018 (companion entries). Treat Latchoumane as the mechanistic base case for 'phase-coupled carrier rhythms gate write landing' and the companion papers as the subdivision-selectivity evidence.
mapping.source_context: Mice; head-fixed and freely-behaving closed-loop optogenetic stimulation of thalamic reticular nucleus during natural NREM; hippocampus-dependent object-place recognition task.

confidence_components.notes: Peer-reviewed Cell-press primary data with optogenetic causal manipulation (source_quality high). Mapping fidelity moderate: the paper demonstrates carrier-rhythm gating but stops short of showing per-subdivision routing. Transfer risk low: the SO-spindle-ripple geometry is highly conserved across rodents and humans, and MECH-261's compute-side abstraction is explicit about being a gate over carrier-rhythm products.
